title: Small Business Administration is available at the City Hall Recreation Center for Disaster assistance loans/info.
description: The U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) will be available at the City Hall Recreation Center from 10:30 AM to 7:00 PM, Tuesday, 2/17, through Friday, 2/20/26, to provide information and assistance with Disaster Assistance Loans follow…
content: The U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) will be available at the City Hall Recreation Center from 10:30 AM to 7:00 PM, Tuesday, 2/17, through Friday, 2/20/26, to provide information and assistance with Disaster Assistance Loans following the recent high tide flooding. SBA representatives can help residents and businesses apply for low-interest disaster loans, explain eligibility requirements, and answer questions about available recovery programs. Additional information and online applications are available at sba.gov/funding-programs/disaster-assistance.

title: Emergency Notification News
description: Start the new year safer! Now is the perfect time to replace the batteries in all older smoke and carbon monoxide detectors—make this an annual habit. Newer smoke and CO₂ detectors are sealed with 10-year batteries, so check the manufactur…
content: Start the new year safer! Now is the perfect time to replace the batteries in all older smoke and carbon monoxide detectors—make this an annual habit. Newer smoke and CO₂ detectors are sealed with 10-year batteries, so check the manufacture date to be sure yours are still within their lifespan. While you’re in safety mode, verify that you are signed up for emergency alerts through AlertMarin and confirm that your contact information is current. AlertMarin is the only official emergency notification system for Belvedere and Tiburon. Other systems like Nixle do not send disaster alerts for the Tiburon Peninsula. However, Nixle may be useful for receiving traffic updates along the Highway 101 corridor. These free services provide critical, real-time notifications during emergencies. For real-time emergency information, visit the Marin County Emergency Portal .

title: Score Big on Safety: Celebrate Super Bowl Responsibly
description: Plan ahead with a sober ride before the big game As football fans gear up for the excitement of the big game, Novato Police reminds everyone that the best game plan includes getting home safely. Whether you're hosting a party, attending a…
content: Plan ahead with a sober ride before the big game As football fans gear up for the excitement of the big game, Novato Police reminds everyone that the best game plan includes getting home safely. Whether you're hosting a party, attending a gathering, or enjoying the game at your favorite spot, planning ahead with a sober ride home will keep you and everyone on the road safe. “Super Bowl celebrations are a time for fun and community, but safety should always come first,” said Sergeant Trevor Hall “If you plan to drink, make sure you have a reliable way to get home, whether that’s a designated sober driver, rideshare, taxi or public transportation.” During Super Bowl LIX weekend, Novato Police will have officers on patrol looking for drivers suspected of being under the influence of alcohol and/or drugs. Plan for a Safe Celebration Before heading out for Super Bowl festivities, follow these tips to stay safe:

title: Tiburon Police Department Arrests Burglary Suspect
description: FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE Case Number TP26-36 & TP26-37 Tiburon, CA -- On 02/04/2026 at 0027 hours, the Tiburon Police Department responded to the 1700 block of Lagoon View Drive for a reported burglary in progress. The homeowner reported that…
content: FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE Case Number TP26-36 & TP26-37 Tiburon, CA -- On 02/04/2026 at 0027 hours, the Tiburon Police Department responded to the 1700 block of Lagoon View Drive for a reported burglary in progress. The homeowner reported that a male suspect entered his garage and stole a bicycle valued at approximately $10,000 before fleeing on foot. The homeowner pursued the suspect, who abandoned the bicycle approximately 50 yards away and fled. The homeowner recovered the bicycle and provided officers with a detailed suspect description. With assistance from the Marin County Sheriff’s Office, Mill Valley Police Department, and Belvedere Police Department, a perimeter was established and an area search was conducted. Officers located scattered coins, a white hat, and two vehicles in the area that appeared to have been rifled through. At approximately 0152 hours, Tiburon officers located a suspicious vehicle parked on Ridge Road. Officers contacted the male occupant who provided inconsistent statements. Officers noted the subject was wearing clothing consistent with the suspect description provided by the victim and observed on surveillance video. The suspect was positively identified and placed under arrest. A search of the suspect’s vehicle revealed suspected methamphetamine, multiple coins, eight stolen credit cards belonging to another Tiburon resident, and additional evidence linked to nearby thefts from unlocked vehicles. The suspect was booked into the Marin County Jail for burglary, possession of a controlled substance, and possession of stolen property. Due to his prior criminal history and convictions, the suspect is eligible for several enhancements pursuant to Proposition 36. This case has been forwarded to the Marin County District Attorney’s Office. As a reminder, the Tiburon Police Department encourages community members to remain vigilant and report suspicious activity. Residents are reminded to lock and secure their vehicles and homes and take additional precautions to safeguard their property. MEDIA CONTACT: Sergeant Rob Law #303 415-706-0661 RLaw@tiburonpd.org

title: UPDATE- The early morning threat received at Redwood High School has been safely resolved without injury or incident.
description: Earlier this morning at approximately 8am, the Central Marin Police received a call from the staff at Redwood High School advising of a possible threat to the school. Staff advised that an unknown caller left a recorded voice mail message…
content: Earlier this morning at approximately 8am, the Central Marin Police received a call from the staff at Redwood High School advising of a possible threat to the school. Staff advised that an unknown caller left a recorded voice mail message indicating that they were driving to Redwood with a gun and a bomb. Officers immediately responded to the school, sent officers into the school, and established a high visibility perimeter around the campus. Within minutes officers coordinated with district administration to begin evacuating the students that were already on campus. With the assistance of our local partners from the Tamalpais Union High School District, Redwood High School, the Central Marin Fire Department, and the Marin County Sheriff's Office, we were able to evacuate the campus in a matter of minutes without injury or incident. The Central Marin Police conducted an extensive search of the entire Redwood High School campus and determined the campus is safe and no threat exists to the safety of the students or staff. The Central Marin Police take these types of threats very seriously and worked collaboratively to ensure the safety of everyone on campus this morning. We recognize how frightening these events are for our residents and school community and stand alongside you to ensure the safety of our children, schools and communities. The Central Marin Police would like to extend our gratitude to the Tamalpais Union High School District, Redwood High School, the Central Marin Fire Department, and the Marin County Sheriff's Office for their partnership and resources in resolving this incident. The Central Marin Police is currently investigating this criminal threat and following up on leads. When located, the suspect/s responsible will be arrested and prosecuted to the fullest extent of the law.

title: Annual Firearm Safety Reminder from the Belvedere P.D.
description: Securely storing firearms when not in use is an essential part of responsible gun ownership—and the most effective way to help prevent firearm accidents, theft, and misuse. Belvedere Municipal Code 9.7 requires all firearms to be properly…
content: Securely storing firearms when not in use is an essential part of responsible gun ownership—and the most effective way to help prevent firearm accidents, theft, and misuse. Belvedere Municipal Code 9.7 requires all firearms to be properly secured when not in use, and California Penal Code 25100 makes an individual criminally liable if a loaded firearm is stored where a child is likely to gain access. The Belvedere Police Department offers free trigger locks and cable locks that prevent a firearm from operating while secured under lock and key, providing an added level of safety. To obtain a lock, call the Belvedere Police Department at (415) 435-3233 to arrange a time with a police officer. For more information on firearm safety, visit the Belvedere P olice Department webpage .

title: High Tides and Heavy Rain Expected - Use Caution and Plan Alternate Routes
description: Attention Corte Madera and Larkspur Residents: Lucky Drive and Fifer Avenue in Corte Madera through Doherty Drive at Riviera Circle in Larkspur remains closed at this time due to flooding. Redwood Highway near Trader Joe's is also presentl…
content: Attention Corte Madera and Larkspur Residents: Lucky Drive and Fifer Avenue in Corte Madera through Doherty Drive at Riviera Circle in Larkspur remains closed at this time due to flooding. Redwood Highway near Trader Joe's is also presently closed. Tomorrow: Please plan alternate routes and expect road closures tomorrow, January 3rd, in flood areas in our jurisdiction. Lucky Drive/Fifer Avenue in Corte Madera through Doherty Drive in Larkspur at Riviera Circle was closed most of the morning and afternoon today. Redwood Highway near Trader Joe's also needed to be closed due to the flooding today. The Golden Hind Passage area in Corte Madera also has experienced heavy flooding. High tide is approximately 11AM tomorrow and will pair with heavy rainfall. Please use caution! Thank you for your assistance and cooperation.

title: Scam Alert: Fradulent Calls from "Tiburon Police"
description: We want our Tiburon community to be aware of a troubling scam that's been making the rounds. Several residents have recently reported receiving phone calls from individuals falsely claiming to represent the Tiburon Police Department. These…
content: We want our Tiburon community to be aware of a troubling scam that's been making the rounds. Several residents have recently reported receiving phone calls from individuals falsely claiming to represent the Tiburon Police Department. These callers tell recipients they've missed a court date or have an outstanding warrant. These calls are fraudulent. The Tiburon Police Department will never contact you by phone demanding that you turn yourself in, nor will they ever request payment in any form—whether cash, gift cards, or cryptocurrency. If you receive one of these suspicious calls, the best course of action is simple: hang up immediately, do not provide any personal information or send money. If you have concerns about the legitimacy of the call or want to verify any information, please contact the Tiburon Police Department directly at (415) 789-2801. Your safety and security are our top priorities.
